name: Almanzora Cycle Street Loop
name_original: Cuevas del Almanzora — circular por las orillas de río
description: This easy 17.3-mile touring cycling route follows the Almanzora river banks, offering pleasant views and access to historical landmarks.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cuevas Del Almanzora features a diverse landscape, from the flat terrain along the Almanzora River to coastal paths offering sea views. The region includes areas of semi-desert alongside green vegetation, providing varied scenery for cyclists. Hills, such as the one hosting the Castillo del Marqués de los Vélez, offer elevated perspectives. The area's geology includes unique formations and ancient cave dwellings, contributing to its distinct character. Best touring cycling routes around Cuevas Del Almanzora The most popular touring cycling route is Almanzora Cycle Street Loop, a 17.3 miles (27.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the course of the Almanzora River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Playa de Quita Pellejos – Carros Salt Flat loop from Villaricos, an easy 13.4 miles (21.5 km) path. This route explores coastal areas near fine sandy beaches. Local touring cyclists also love the Cala Cristal Views – Coastal Path With Sea Views loop from Los Lobos, a 36.4 miles (58.6 km) trail leading through coastal paths with expansive sea views, often completed in about 3 hours 38 minutes. Touring cycling around Cuevas Del Almanzora is defined by the Almanzora River, coastal paths, and varied terrain including semi-desert and green patches. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside routes to more challenging coastal climbs. The routes in Cuevas Del Almanzora are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cuevas Del Almanzora's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cuevas Del Almanzora?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You'll find relatively flat terrain along the Almanzora River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047484890/almanzora-cycle-street-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanzora Cycle Street Loop&lt;/a&gt;, as well as coastal paths with sea views. There are also areas of semi-desert alongside green vegetation, and some routes include hills, offering varied experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Cuevas Del Almanzora has several easy touring cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047484890/almanzora-cycle-street-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanzora Cycle Street Loop&lt;/a&gt;, following the Almanzora River, is an easy 17.3-mile path. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31914694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Quita Pellejos – Carros Salt Flat loop from Villaricos&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 13.4-mile route exploring coastal areas near fine sandy beaches. These are great choices for those looking for less challenging rides.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I discover along the touring cycling routes?
A: You can explore a variety of natural features and historical landmarks. The Almanzora River bed is a prominent feature, and coastal routes offer views of quiet, fine sandy beaches. You might also encounter unique geological formations like the prehistoric enclave of Calguerín with its caves and palm trees. For historical sights, consider routes that pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5329205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Marquis of Los Vélez&lt;/a&gt; or the fascinating ancient cave dwellings.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cuevas Del Almanzora?
A: The region's climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer. Winter can also be a good option, with milder temperatures compared to many other European regions, though some routes might be affected by seasonal conditions.

Q: Are there many circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Cuevas Del Almanzora are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047484890/almanzora-cycle-street-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almanzora Cycle Street Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Cristal Views – Coastal Path With Sea Views loop from Los Lobos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers expansive sea views.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Cuevas Del Almanzora?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 50 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from easy riverside paths to challenging coastal climbs, and the varied scenery that includes semi-desert landscapes and green patches of vegetation. The combination of physical activity and cultural enrichment from historical sites is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for more experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Path With Sea Views – Cala Cristal Views loop from Los Lobos&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 44.1-mile trail with significant elevation changes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38280214" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mojácar Village – Carros Salt Flat loop from Cuevas del Almanzora&lt;/a&gt;, covering 39.8 miles with substantial climbs.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Cuevas Del Almanzora?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes can vary, many natural outdoor areas in Cuevas Del Almanzora are generally welcoming to dogs, especially if kept on a leash. It's always best to ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them. Consider easier, less crowded routes, particularly those along the Almanzora River, for a more comfortable experience with your canine companion.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or attractions near the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the routes themselves, you can explore several attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2093636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas del Almanzora Castle&lt;/a&gt; offers historical insight, while natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cocedores Beach&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4282404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salar de los Caños Wetland&lt;/a&gt; provide scenic stops. The region also features unique cave homes, offering a glimpse into local history and architecture.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are there in Cuevas Del Almanzora?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available in Cuevas Del Almanzora, catering to various skill levels. This includes 12 easy routes, 4 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes, ensuring a wide range of options for every touring cyclist.

Q: Are there any coastal touring cycling routes with sea views?
A: Yes, Cuevas Del Almanzora offers beautiful coastal touring cycling routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Cristal Views – Coastal Path With Sea Views loop from Los Lobos&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing expansive sea views along its 36.4-mile path. These routes often pass by quiet beaches like those found near Palomares, perfect for a break.
tips: The Cuevas del Calguerín are a testimony of human settlement in the area since prehistoric times. Known for their Levantine-style cave paintings, these caves have been used by different cultures over the centuries. Thanks to its restoration and adaptation for tourism, today it is possible to explore and better understand the historical and cultural wealth of the region.

The path runs along the banks of the Almanzora river, offering a beautiful landscape for nature lovers. Throughout the route you can enjoy the course of the river, the native vegetation and the local fauna. Upon reaching the Almería coast, the reward is beautiful views of the Mediterranean Sea.

Fortress built in the 16th century. It was built by the Marquis de los Vélez, Pedro Fajardo, as a symbol of his power and wealth in the area.

The castle is a magnificent example of Spanish Renaissance architecture and is made up of two parts: the fortress and the palace. The fortress consists of two towers and a defensive wall, while the palace presents an impressive façade with decorative elements in the Gothic, Renaissance and Mudejar styles.

The castle has been recently restored and currently houses a museum dedicated to the archeology and history of the area. You can also enjoy panoramic views from the top of the towers and explore the network of underground tunnels that connect different parts of the castle.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cuevas del Almanzora river banks cycling route?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route around Cuevas del Almanzora. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the town itself is a good starting point. You can likely find parking within the town, which is a charming Spanish market town, and access the route from there.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is rated as easy and is ideal for beginners and families. It requires minimal fitness, features mostly paved surfaces, and has a modest elevation gain, making it a relaxed and manageable ride for a wide range of cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cuevas del Almanzora river banks trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this circular route is predominantly paved, making it very suitable for touring bicycles. It follows the banks of the Almanzora river, offering a smooth and comfortable ride without significant climbs.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 27.9 kilometers long. It can typically be completed in about 1 hour and 38 minutes, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Almería, where Cuevas del Almanzora is located, boasts over 3,000 hours of sunshine annually and minimal rain. This makes it an attractive destination for cycling almost year-round. However, summer months can be very hot, so spring and autumn are often ideal for more comfortable temperatures.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy the natural beauty of the Almanzora river banks. Notable points of interest nearby or along the path include the Almanzora Cycle Street, the historical Cuevas del Calguerín with their ancient cave paintings, and the impressive Castle of the Marquis of Los Vélez. You might also catch views of the Cuevas del Almanzora Reservoir.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail follows natural river banks and dedicated cycleways, specific rules regarding dogs are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash in natural areas and ensure you clean up after them. Check local regulations for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the "Cuevas del Almanzora — circular by the river banks" route. It is an open public cycling path.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the river banks?
faqs.answer: The natural areas along the Almanzora river are characterized by bushy, herbaceous, and non-forest vegetation. You might spot various birds, small mammals, and potentially even the endangered Greek tortoise. The Cuevas del Almanzora Reservoir, if visible, is also known for birds like herons and cormorants.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route utilize parts of the official Ciclocalle Almanzora. It also briefly intersects with the Ciclovia Almanzora, Canada de Lorca, and Calle Torres.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: The route itself follows river banks and dedicated cycleways, so direct facilities like cafes or restrooms might be limited along the immediate path. However, the route is circular and starts/ends near the town of Cuevas del Almanzora, where you will find various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and shops.

waypoints.name: Castle of the Marquis of Los Vélez
